leagueBriefing: Saturday was a day of absolute offensive dominance across the country, highlighted by Mississippi State's historic 26-run outburst and several run-rule routs by top-ranked programs. While UCLA and Florida State flexed their muscles with double-digit wins, the day also featured extra-inning drama in Chapel Hill and Morgantown, proving that no lead was safe in a wild day for college baseball.

games.headline: No. 1 UCLA demolishes Ohio State in 19-run explosion
games.brief: The top-ranked Bruins left no doubt about their status, overwhelming Ohio State with a relentless 14-hit attack. UCLA scored in almost every inning, turning a competitive early game into a lopsided affair that was eventually halted by the run rule in the eighth.
bullets: UCLA's offense was headlined by four-RBI performances from both Mulivai Levu and Dean West.
Reliever Wylan Moss was a stabilizer, throwing five scoreless innings with eight strikeouts.
Will Gasparino paced the hit parade with a 4-for-5 day at the plate.
The Bruins hit three home runs in the contest to power the blowout.
games.context: UCLA continues to justify its No. 1 ranking with a dominant showing in Big Ten play.

games.headline: Sacramento State stuns No. 2 LSU in Baton Rouge
games.brief: Sacramento State pulled off a massive upset at Alex Box Stadium, handing No. 2 LSU a rare home loss. The Hornets used timely hitting and a resilient bullpen to hold off a late Tiger rally, silencing the home crowd in a signature win for the program.
bullets: LSU was limited to just three hits in the entire contest.
Sacramento State took advantage of a pivotal three-run inning to build their lead.
LSU's highly-touted pitching staff struggled with command, walking six Hornets.
The win marks one of the biggest upsets in Sacramento State history.
games.context: This loss is a major wake-up call for an LSU team that had been rolling through non-conference play.

games.headline: Texas fends off USC Upstate in high-scoring shootout
games.brief: In a back-and-forth slugfest at Disch-Falk Field, the Longhorns outlasted a feisty USC Upstate squad. Texas relied on their big bats to reclaim the lead late after the Spartans briefly threatened an upset in the middle innings.
bullets: Texas hit three home runs to offset 14 hits by USC Upstate.
The Longhorns' bullpen secured the final six outs without allowing a run.
Texas remains a perfect 14-0 on the season with this victory.
The Spartans had the tying run at the plate in the top of the ninth.
games.context: Texas preserves their undefeated record despite a defensive struggle.

games.headline: Mississippi State makes history in 26-run demolition of Lipscomb
games.brief: No. 4 Mississippi State put on an offensive display for the ages, scoring 26 runs in just seven innings. The Bulldogs were relentless from the jump, matching the historic onslaught with a combined one-hitter on the mound in a performance that left the SEC reeling.
bullets: The 26 runs are the most scored by Mississippi State in a single game since 2010.
Noah Sullivan powered the offense with two home runs and six RBIs.
Duke Stone was untouchable, throwing four perfect innings to start the one-hitter.
MSU finished with 20 hits, including 10 for extra bases.
games.context: A statement win for Mississippi State that serves notice to the rest of the SEC.

games.headline: Georgia Tech hammers Hokies behind 18-hit barrage
games.brief: The Yellow Jackets' offense was firing on all cylinders in a rout of ACC rival Virginia Tech. Georgia Tech used a massive eighth-run inning to put the game out of reach, turning a close contest into a landslide victory.
bullets: Vahn Lackey was the star for GT, driving in five runs on three hits.
Georgia Tech finished with 18 hits, spreading the wealth across the entire lineup.
Virginia Tech's pitching struggled, yielding eight runs in a nightmare seventh inning.
The win clinches the series for the Yellow Jackets.
games.context: Georgia Tech makes a strong early impression in ACC play with an offensive showcase.

games.headline: Dietz's 12 strikeouts lead Arkansas to run-rule win
games.brief: No. 6 Arkansas rode a dominant performance from Hunter Dietz to cruise past Stetson. Dietz carved up the Hatters' lineup before the Razorback bats exploded late to trigger the run rule and secure a comfortable Saturday victory.
bullets: Hunter Dietz set a career high with 12 strikeouts over just six innings.
The Hogs launched three home runs, including a towering shot to center field.
Arkansas has now won 35 consecutive non-conference home series.
Stetson was held to just four hits in the seven-inning game.
games.context: Arkansas continues its historic home dominance in non-conference weekend series.

games.headline: Sanders' 10 strikeouts power Auburn to shutout in Game 1
games.brief: Jackson Sanders was the story for the Tigers, baffling Winthrop hitters throughout the series opener. Auburn's defense was equally impressive, turning two key double plays to support Sanders' double-digit strikeout performance.
bullets: Jackson Sanders threw seven dominant shutout innings with 10 strikeouts.
Chris Rembert paced the offense with three hits and two RBIs.
Auburn jumped out to an early 3-0 lead in the first inning and never looked back.
The Tigers' pitchers allowed only three hits in the shutout.
games.context: Auburn's pitching staff continues to be one of the most reliable in the SEC.

games.headline: Auburn completes sweep of Winthrop after rain-delayed Game 2
games.brief: Despite a weather delay and an early deficit, No. 7 Auburn showed their depth to complete the doubleheader sweep of Winthrop. The Tigers used a steady offensive attack to grind down the Eagles and secure the series victory.
bullets: Mason McCraine led the Tigers with three RBIs in the nightcap.
Taylor Belza provided the power with his first career home run.
Auburn pitchers surrendered only one run across the entire 18 innings of play Saturday.
The win moves Auburn to 13-2 on the season.
games.context: Auburn completes a perfect Saturday, showcasing elite pitching depth.

games.headline: Virginia cruises past Tar Heels in ACC rivalry opener
games.brief: Virginia's bats came alive early in Chapel Hill, silencing the North Carolina faithful in a lopsided series opener. The Cavaliers used a multi-run fourth inning to build a cushion and never let the Tar Heels back into the contest.
bullets: Virginia's starters combined for 12 hits, keeping the pressure on all afternoon.
The Tar Heels were limited to just four hits in the loss.
Virginia pitchers stranded eight Tar Heel runners on base.
This win marks a strong start to ACC play for the Cavaliers.
games.context: Virginia takes the first punch in a crucial early-season ACC rivalry series.

games.headline: Tar Heels salvage split with 12-inning walk-off thriller
games.brief: In a game that lasted nearly four hours and 12 grueling innings, North Carolina found a way to even the series. After blowing a late lead, the Tar Heels showed immense grit to walk it off in extras and salvage the Saturday split.
bullets: Howe delivered the game-winning walk-off hit in the bottom of the 12th inning.
The Tar Heels overcame four defensive errors to stay in the game.
The teams combined for 24 hits in the marathon contest.
The series will be decided in a high-stakes Sunday rubber match.
games.context: A character-building win for UNC that prevents a devastating home series loss.

games.headline: High Point stuns No. 9 Florida to clinch shock series
games.brief: High Point followed up their Friday night upset with another dominant showing, handing No. 9 Florida a series loss at Condron Family Ballpark. The Gators' offense went cold for the second straight day as High Point's pitching held them to just two runs.
bullets: High Point has now won back-to-back games against a top-10 opponent.
Florida's 13-game winning streak was effectively buried by the Panthers.
The Gators were held to six hits and struggled with runners in scoring position.
High Point pitchers combined for nine strikeouts in the win.
games.context: A massive weekend for High Point that will reverberate through the RPI rankings.

games.headline: No. 10 Southern Miss pulls away late to sink Lions
games.brief: In a game that was closer than the final score suggests, Southern Miss used a four-run explosion in the eighth inning to secure a series win. The Golden Eagles' pitching kept North Alabama at bay until the offense finally found its rhythm late.
bullets: Southern Miss recorded its 13th win of the season.
The Golden Eagles were held to just five hits but made them count with timely power.
UNA's Luke Davenport pitched a career-high 5.1 innings in the losing effort.
The series sweep is now on the line for Southern Miss on Sunday.
games.context: Southern Miss continues to prove why they are a top-10 mainstay.

games.headline: Branch's two homers lead Georgia to Game 1 blowout
games.brief: Georgia's Kolby Branch put on a clinic in the doubleheader opener, launching two home runs to spark a double-digit victory. The Bulldogs' offense was relentless, scoring in six different innings to overwhelm Queens.
bullets: Kolby Branch finished with five RBIs, including a grand slam.
Georgia hit four home runs as a team in the first contest.
Caden Aoki earned the win with five solid innings on the mound.
The win moved Georgia to 13-3 on the season.
games.context: Georgia's offense is reaching elite levels as they enter the meat of their schedule.

games.headline: Vigue’s 11 Ks and walk-off homer secure UGA sweep
games.brief: The nightcap was all about Dylan Vigue and Kolby Branch. Vigue dominated on the mound with 11 strikeouts, and Branch capped off a historic day with a walk-off three-run homer to trigger the run rule and secure a milestone win for Coach Wes Johnson.
bullets: Dylan Vigue allowed only one hit over seven dominant shutout innings.
Kolby Branch hit a walk-off 3-run HR, his third homer of the day.
Brennan Hudson added a rare inside-the-park home run in the win.
The victory was the 100th career win for head coach Wes Johnson.
games.context: A career milestone for Coach Johnson in a perfect day of baseball for the Bulldogs.

games.headline: Mercurius’ gem clinches series for No. 12 Oklahoma
games.brief: The Sooners pitching staff was the star of the show, shutting out Santa Clara to clinch the weekend series. LJ Mercurius was dominant, allowing the Oklahoma bats to work patiently and build a lead that was never threatened.
bullets: LJ Mercurius threw six scoreless innings with six strikeouts and zero walks.
Brenden Brock provided the offensive spark with a key two-RBI double.
Oklahoma pitching limited Santa Clara to just four hits in the game.
This marks the third consecutive series win for the Sooners.
games.context: Oklahoma's pitching depth is proving to be a massive asset early in the season.

games.headline: Dudan’s 10 strikeouts pace NC State in bounce-back win
games.brief: No. 13 NC State answered an early-series upset with a commanding victory over Lafayette. Jacob Dudan was electric on the mound, keeping the Leopards off balance while the Wolfpack offense provided steady run support to even the weekend.
bullets: Jacob Dudan set the tone with 10 strikeouts in 6.2 innings.
Ty Head led the offense with a home run and five RBIs.
The Wolfpack scored in four of the first five innings to take control.
NC State pitchers surrendered only five hits in the contest.
games.context: NC State recovers quickly to keep their series hopes alive.

games.headline: Clemson holds off late La Salle rally in Game 1
games.brief: No. 14 Clemson used an early offensive cushion and some solid relief pitching to take the first game of their Saturday doubleheader. La Salle made it interesting late, but the Tigers' defense held firm to preserve the win.
bullets: Purify led the Tigers with a home run and two RBIs.
La Salle hit two home runs in a late-inning attempt to close the gap.
Clemson's bullpen recorded the final six outs with four strikeouts.
The win moved Clemson to 14-1 on the season.
games.context: Clemson continues their nearly flawless start to the 2026 campaign.

games.headline: Clemson completes sweep with 16-run offensive clinic
games.brief: The Tigers left no room for drama in the nightcap, dismantling La Salle in a seven-inning run-rule rout. A massive eight-run fourth inning broke the game wide open, allowing Clemson to showcase their deep bench in the blowout sweep.
bullets: Clemson's offense exploded for eight runs in a single inning.
Every starter in the Clemson lineup recorded at least one hit.
The game was called after seven innings due to the run rule.
Clemson has outscored opponents by over 50 runs in their last five games.
games.context: Clemson is entering ACC play as one of the hottest teams in the country.

games.headline: Wake Forest holds off Cardinal in high-scoring thriller
games.brief: No. 15 Wake Forest outslugged Stanford in a contest that felt more like a Home Run Derby than an ACC matchup. The Demon Deacons built a significant lead early and had enough insurance to withstand a desperate ninth-inning rally by the Cardinal.
bullets: Luke Costello paced the Wake Forest attack with a home run and four RBIs.
Stanford hit three home runs but struggled to keep pace with the Deacs' depth.
The teams combined for 24 hits in the first game of the day.
Wake Forest moved to 15-1 with the hard-fought win.
games.context: Wake Forest proves they can win the high-scoring slugfests that define modern college baseball.

games.headline: Deacons complete sweep as Costello hits two more homers
games.brief: Wake Forest completed the series sweep of Stanford in another high-scoring affair. Luke Costello finished off a legendary day by launching two more home runs, totaling three on the day, as the Demon Deacons proved to be too much for the Cardinal in Winston-Salem.
bullets: Luke Costello hit two home runs and drove in five in the nightcap.
Stanford hit an incredible six home runs in the loss.
Wake Forest swept the three-game series against the perennially strong Cardinal.
The Demon Deacons have now won 10 straight games.
games.context: A statement sweep for Wake Forest that solidifies their status as ACC favorites.

games.headline: Watson’s late heroics lead No. 16 Coastal over ECU
games.brief: For the second straight day, Rex Watson was the hero for Coastal Carolina. After a walk-off grand slam on Friday, Watson delivered a tie-breaking three-run homer in the eighth inning to spark a six-run outburst that sank East Carolina.
bullets: Rex Watson's go-ahead home run in the eighth was his second game-changing hit in 24 hours.
Coastal Carolina scored six runs in a single frame to break open a 1-1 tie.
ECU was held to just one run on five hits.
The win marks a signature series victory for the Chanticleers.
games.context: Coastal Carolina establishes itself as a regional powerhouse with a series win over a top rival.

games.headline: Oregon State scores seven straight to cruise past Xavier
games.brief: No. 18 Oregon State overcame an early deficit with a steady, disciplined offensive performance. The Beavers' pitching staff locked down after the first inning, allowing the lineup to methodically build a lead that Xavier could never touch.
bullets: Oregon State scored seven unanswered runs after falling behind 2-0 early.
Jacob Galloway reached base five times and drove in three runs.
The Beavers have now won four consecutive games.
Oregon State pitching struck out 11 Xavier batters in the win.
games.context: The Beavers are finding their rhythm as they approach the start of Pac-12 play.

games.headline: No. 19 Tennessee edges Wright State in nail-biter
games.brief: The Volunteers had to dig deep to survive a Saturday scare against Wright State. Tennessee relied on a pair of timely home runs and some clutch relief pitching to escape with a one-run victory and keep their series winning streak alive.
bullets: Henry Ford and Reese Chapman both homered in the win.
Reese Chapman led the team with three hits and three RBIs.
The Vols overcame an early 2-0 deficit in the third inning.
The Tennessee bullpen threw three scoreless innings to close out the game.
games.context: Tennessee avoids a potential resume-denting upset at home.

games.headline: FSU showcases power and speed in run-rule rout
games.brief: No. 20 Florida State put on a show in Tallahassee, using a mix of traditional power and aggressive baserunning to dismantle Northern Kentucky. The Seminoles reached the run rule early, punctuating the win with a rare steal of home that sent the crowd into a frenzy.
bullets: Putnam launched a massive grand slam to highlight the offensive outburst.
Sheffield recorded a rare steal of home in the fourth inning.
The game was called after seven innings due to the run rule.
FSU clinched the weekend series with the dominant win.
games.context: FSU's aggressive style of play is making them one of the most exciting teams in the country.

games.headline: Kentucky rallies late to overcome The Citadel
games.brief: Kentucky's offense came through when it mattered most, scoring five runs in the eighth inning to flip the script against The Citadel. After trailing late, the Wildcats used a barrage of extra-base hits to secure a gritty home victory.
bullets: Kentucky scored five runs in a decisive eighth-inning rally.
Jayce Tharnish and Ethan Hindle both homered during the late surge.
Tyler Bell was a constant presence on base, reaching four times.
The win moves Kentucky to 13-2 on the season.
games.context: A character-building rally for Kentucky that keeps their momentum high heading into SEC play.

games.headline: No. 22 Texas A&M completes series sweep in run-rule fashion
games.brief: The Aggies left no doubt in their series finale against Oakland, finishing off a three-game sweep with a convincing run-rule victory. Texas A&M's deep lineup hammered out 14 hits, providing more than enough support for a lights-out pitching performance.
bullets: Texas A&M recorded its third series sweep of the young season.
Caden Sorrell and Blake Binderup both homered and drove in three runs.
The Aggies' pitching staff allowed only one run on eight scattered hits.
The game was halted after seven innings by the run rule.
games.context: Texas A&M enters the national conversation as a dominant force after another series sweep.

games.headline: Kesser’s 10th-inning grand slam gives WVU walk-off win
games.brief: In a game that required extra innings after a late Columbia rally, No. 23 West Virginia found heroics from Brodie Kesser. Kesser stepped to the plate with the bases loaded in the 10th and delivered the ultimate walk-off—a moonshot grand slam to secure the victory.
bullets: Brodie Kesser's walk-off grand slam was the first of its kind for WVU this season.
Columbia had fought back from a 3-0 deficit to force the extra frames.
West Virginia recorded 15 hits in the back-and-forth contest.
Jackson Brewer paced Columbia with two hits and two RBIs.
games.context: A thrilling signature win for West Virginia that reinforces their top-25 status.

games.headline: No. 24 Miami holds off late BC rally to even series
games.brief: Miami used a fast start to build a lead and just enough late-inning pitching to survive a Boston College comeback attempt. The Hurricanes evened the ACC series at one apiece, setting up a high-stakes Sunday rubber match in Coral Gables.
bullets: Miami jumped out to a 3-0 lead in the very first inning.
The Hurricanes hit back-to-back solo home runs in the middle innings.
Boston College brought the tying run to the plate in both the eighth and ninth innings.
Miami's bullpen struck out the side in the ninth to secure the win.
games.context: Miami recovers from a Friday loss to stay in the thick of the ACC race.

games.headline: UTSA ties program record with dominant 13-0 shutout
games.brief: UTSA reached a historic milestone in emphatic fashion, dismantling New Mexico State to move to 13-1 on the year. The Roadrunners' pitching was nearly perfect, allowing only three hits in a seven-inning shutout that tied the school record for best start through 14 games.
bullets: UTSA tied the 2008 team for the best start in program history (13-1).
Conor Myles dominated on the mound with eight strikeouts in five innings.
Lane Haworth led the hit parade with a 4-for-5 performance.
The game was called after seven innings due to the run rule.
games.context: UTSA matches a historic program record, signaling a potentially special season in San Antonio.
